title: Database-driven protein solubilization
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7l98j","text":"This is a protocol for using the extraction database found at www.polymerscreen.yale.edu for optimal extraction of membrane proteins into native nanodiscs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Visit www.polymerscreen.yale.edu to search for your protein in the extraction database. This will provide you with the polymer to use for optimal protein extraction.
As an example, below are representative organellar markers and the optimal polymer for extraction: TMEM192 --> AASTY650 TGN46 --> ChloroSMA60 OMP25 --> SMA200 VAPA --> AASTY650 KRas --> ChloroSMA80
Once the optimal polymer has been determined, express the protein of interest, or harvest cells for endogenous solubilization.
Resuspend cellular membranes in the chosen polymer and incubate at 4 °C with rotation for 02:00:00 hours.
Ultracentrifuge samples ay 200,000xg for 01:00:00 hours to remove insoluble material.
creator: Caroline Brown
